---
title: "Using pmxSynthData"
author: "Andrew Stein"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: true
    fig_width: 9
    fig_height: 5
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Using pmxSynthData}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", fig.align = "center")
library(pmxSynthData)
has_nlmixr2data <- requireNamespace("nlmixr2data", quietly = TRUE)
has_ggplot2 <- requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)
comparison_colours <- c(Source = "#1B6CA8", Synthetic = "#D95F02")

observed_plot_data <- function(data, roles, dataset) {
  observed <- as.character(data[[roles$evid]]) %in% c("0", "0.0")
  if (!is.null(roles$mdv)) {
    observed <- observed & as.character(data[[roles$mdv]]) %in% c("0", "0.0")
  }
  observed <- observed & !is.na(data[[roles$dv]])
  observation_rows <- which(observed)
  occasion <- rep(1L, length(observation_rows))
  subject_values <- data[[roles$id]]
  for (id in unique(subject_values[observation_rows])) {
    subject_rows <- which(!is.na(subject_values) & subject_values == id)
    events <- !(as.character(data[[roles$evid]][subject_rows]) %in%
                  c("0", "0.0"))
    if (!is.null(roles$amt)) {
      events <- events & as.numeric(data[[roles$amt]][subject_rows]) > 0
    }
    dose_times <- sort(unique(as.numeric(data[[roles$time]][
      subject_rows[events]
    ])))
    positions <- which(subject_values[observation_rows] == id)
    if (length(dose_times)) {
      occasion[positions] <- pmax(1L, findInterval(
        as.numeric(data[[roles$time]][observation_rows[positions]]),
        dose_times
      ))
    }
  }
  data.frame(
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("Source", "Synthetic")),
    subject = as.character(data[[roles$id]][observation_rows]),
    time = as.numeric(data[[roles$time]][observation_rows]),
    dv = as.numeric(data[[roles$dv]][observation_rows]),
    occasion = occasion,
    endpoint = if (is.null(roles$dvid)) "DV" else
      as.character(data[[roles$dvid]][observation_rows]),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

tad_plot_data <- function(data, roles, dataset) {
  observed <- as.character(data[[roles$evid]]) %in% c("0", "0.0") &
    !is.na(data[[roles$dv]])
  ids <- data[[roles$id]]
  out <- vector("list", length(unique(ids)))
  for (i in seq_along(unique(ids))) {
    id <- unique(ids)[i]
    subject_rows <- which(ids == id)
    event <- !(as.character(data[[roles$evid]][subject_rows]) %in%
                 c("0", "0.0"))
    if (!is.null(roles$amt)) {
      event <- event & as.numeric(data[[roles$amt]][subject_rows]) > 0
    }
    dose_times <- sort(unique(as.numeric(
      data[[roles$time]][subject_rows[event]]
    )))
    rows <- subject_rows[observed[subject_rows]]
    occasion <- pmax(1L, findInterval(
      as.numeric(data[[roles$time]][rows]), dose_times
    ))
    occasion <- pmin(occasion, length(dose_times))
    out[[i]] <- data.frame(
      dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("Source", "Synthetic")),
      subject = as.character(id), occasion = occasion,
      tad = as.numeric(data[[roles$time]][rows]) - dose_times[occasion],
      dv = as.numeric(data[[roles$dv]][rows]), endpoint = "DV",
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
  do.call(rbind, out)
}

demo_design_summary <- function(data, roles, dataset, time_bounds) {
  plotted <- observed_plot_data(data, roles, dataset)
  plotted <- plotted[
    plotted$time >= time_bounds[1L] & plotted$time <= time_bounds[2L],
    , drop = FALSE
  ]
  cohort_ids <- as.character(unique(data[[roles$id]]))
  pieces <- lapply(sort(unique(plotted$endpoint)), function(endpoint) {
    rows <- plotted[plotted$endpoint == endpoint, , drop = FALSE]
    counts <- table(factor(rows$subject, levels = cohort_ids))
    data.frame(
      dataset = dataset,
      endpoint = endpoint,
      patients = length(cohort_ids),
      patients_with_endpoint = sum(counts > 0),
      observations = nrow(rows),
      mean_time_points_per_patient = mean(counts),
      median_time_points_per_patient = stats::median(counts),
      first_time = min(rows$time),
      last_time = max(rows$time),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })
  do.call(rbind, pieces)
}

check_demo_similarity <- function(source, synthetic, roles, time_bounds,
                                  label) {
  source_subjects <- length(unique(source[[roles$id]]))
  synthetic_subjects <- length(unique(synthetic[[roles$id]]))
  if (source_subjects != synthetic_subjects) {
    stop(label, ": source and synthetic patient counts differ (",
         source_subjects, " versus ", synthetic_subjects, ").")
  }
  source_summary <- demo_design_summary(
    source, roles, "Source", time_bounds
  )
  synthetic_summary <- demo_design_summary(
    synthetic, roles, "Synthetic", time_bounds
  )
  if (!setequal(source_summary$endpoint, synthetic_summary$endpoint)) {
    stop(label, ": source and synthetic endpoint sets differ.")
  }
  paired <- merge(
    source_summary, synthetic_summary, by = "endpoint",
    suffixes = c("_source", "_synthetic")
  )
  point_difference <- abs(
    paired$mean_time_points_per_patient_synthetic -
      paired$mean_time_points_per_patient_source
  )
  point_allowance <- pmax(
    1, 0.25 * paired$mean_time_points_per_patient_source
  )
  if (any(point_difference > point_allowance)) {
    stop(label, ": mean time points per patient differ materially for ",
         paste(paired$endpoint[point_difference > point_allowance],
               collapse = ", "), ".")
  }
  coverage_allowance <- 0.20 * diff(time_bounds)
  bad_coverage <-
    abs(paired$first_time_synthetic - paired$first_time_source) >
      coverage_allowance |
    abs(paired$last_time_synthetic - paired$last_time_source) >
      coverage_allowance
  if (any(bad_coverage)) {
    stop(label, ": source and synthetic time coverage differs materially for ",
         paste(paired$endpoint[bad_coverage], collapse = ", "), ".")
  }
  summary <- rbind(source_summary, synthetic_summary)
  summary$dataset <- factor(
    summary$dataset, levels = c("Source", "Synthetic")
  )
  summary[order(summary$dataset, summary$endpoint), , drop = FALSE]
}

comparison_facets <- function(data) {
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(
    ggplot2::vars(dataset, endpoint),
    ncol = max(1L, length(unique(data$endpoint))),
    scales = "free_y"
  )
}
```

## What this package is for

`pmxSynthData` creates source-calibrated **mock data for model-workflow
exploration**. Fit once where the source data are permitted, export the fitted
private population model or data generated solely from it, and generate as
many workflow datasets as needed without consulting the source again.

The goal is broad structural usefulness: PMX joins and derivations, plots,
control-file plumbing, repeated-dose PK code, longitudinal PD/biomarker code,
and censoring conventions. The output is deliberately unsuitable for parameter
estimation, scientific inference, model selection, dose decisions, or claims
that source distributions were reproduced.

The values of `epsilon` and `delta` below are illustrations, not universal
recommendations. Epsilon is a one-person influence limit; smaller is stronger.
Delta is a small additive allowance in the probability bound, not a breach
probability or a fraction of unprotected patients. Choosing either value is a
study- and governance-specific decision.

## What statistical model describes the trajectories?

This package does **not** fit the ODE/NLME model a pharmacometrician might
normally expect. There are no compartments, clearances, volumes, turnover
parameters, individual random effects, or likelihood-based estimation. It also
does not currently fit splines.

Instead, each endpoint uses a deliberately coarse **fixed-grid trajectory
model**. Within each subject, observations are assigned to the nearest cell on
a public dose-relative or study-time grid. Bounded subject-level presence and
cell-mean vectors are released through the configured privacy mechanism. During
generation, adjacent released cell means receive a short 1-2-1 moving-average
smoother and `stats::approx()` performs piecewise-linear interpolation. Public
subject shifts and AR(1) residual perturbations create broad variability.

Sampling is a separate two-part model, not a consequence of the dose regimen.
For every possible dose occasion, fitting releases a bounded probability that
the endpoint is sampled and a bounded observation count conditional on that
occasion being sampled. Thus Q24H dose rows can coexist with no concentration
samples on intervening occasions. `sampling_summary()` displays this fitted
design without consulting the source again.

For a dose-relative endpoint, this empirical curve is reused after generated
doses; a study-time endpoint uses one global curve. Consequently, the mock data
can exercise an ODE/NLME workflow, but they are not draws from the source ODE or
NLME model and should not preserve individual PK parameters, accumulation,
covariate effects, or the exact distribution of profiles.

## Installation and backend check

```{r installation, eval=FALSE}
install.packages("pmxSynthData")
# The production fitter also requires the OpenDP R package:
install.packages("opendp", repos = "https://opendp.r-universe.dev")
```

```{r backend-status}
dp_backend_status()
```

Fitting confidential data fails closed unless the validated OpenDP adapter is
available. It never substitutes ordinary R random noise. This vignette remains
runnable in minimal package-check environments by fitting only fully public
fixtures with `backend = "public", public_source = TRUE`. That explicit mode is
nonprivate and is rejected for confidential inputs.

## Minimal end-to-end workflow

Start with a fully public censoring fixture and expand it to eight simulated
subjects. This is demonstration data, not patient data.

```{r minimal-source}
one <- pmx_censoring_fixture()
public_source <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:8, function(id) {
  subject <- one
  subject$ID <- as.integer(id)
  subject$DV[subject$CENS == 0 & subject$EVID == 0] <-
    subject$DV[subject$CENS == 0 & subject$EVID == 0] + id / 20
  subject
}))
rownames(public_source) <- NULL

roles <- pmx_roles(
  id = "ID", time = "TIME", dv = "DV", amt = "AMT", evid = "EVID",
  dvid = "DVID", mdv = "MDV", cens = "CENS", limit = "LIMIT"
)

endpoints <- list(
  marker = pmx_endpoint(
    dvid = "marker", alignment = "study_time", transform = "identity",
    shape = "global", grid = 1:4,
    censoring = list(left = 1, right = 10, interval = c(2, 4))
  )
)
```

Bounds, schema, factor levels, timing grids, and contribution limits are public
inputs. Establish them from protocol knowledge or an approved public schema,
not by silently taking confidential minima and maxima.

```{r minimal-config}
bounds <- pmx_bounds(
  time = c(0, 5), endpoints = list(marker = c(0, 12)),
  amt = c(0, 200), limit = list(marker = c(0, 12))
)

public_design <- pmx_public_design(
  schema = pmx_schema(public_source),
  dose_times = 0, n_doses = 1, dose_amount = 100, dose_evid = 1,
  endpoint_grids = list(marker = 1:4)
)

contribution_limits <- pmx_contribution_limits(
  max_rows = 8, max_doses = 2, max_occasions = 2,
  max_observations_per_endpoint = 5, max_timing_cells = 4
)

budget <- pmx_budget_allocation(
  subject_count = 0.10, event = 0.10, timing = 0.15,
  covariates = 0, endpoints = 0.45, censoring = 0.20
)
```

The confidential-data call has the same shape, but uses OpenDP and must not set
`public_source = TRUE`:

```{r production-fit-shape, eval=FALSE}
private_model <- fit_private_pmx(
  confidential_data, roles, endpoints,
  epsilon = approved_epsilon,
  delta = approved_delta,
  delta_justification = approved_delta_justification,
  bounds = bounds,
  public_design = public_design,
  contribution_limits = contribution_limits,
  budget_allocation = budget,
  backend = "opendp"
)
```

For this public fixture only:

```{r minimal-fit}
private_model <- suppressWarnings(fit_private_pmx(
  public_source, roles, endpoints,
  epsilon = 3, delta = 0,
  bounds = bounds,
  public_design = public_design,
  contribution_limits = contribution_limits,
  budget_allocation = budget,
  backend = "public", public_source = TRUE
))

privacy_report(private_model)
validate_private_model(private_model)$valid
```

The public-fixture report correctly makes no DP claim. A production OpenDP
report instead states the subject-level `(epsilon, delta)` guarantee, backend
version, contribution bounds, every composed query, public inputs, proof
assumptions, and release-ledger ID.

Generate repeatedly from the same fitted model. When `n_subjects` is omitted,
the generator uses the fitted privacy-accounted cohort count. It is exactly the
source count for these explicitly public fixtures; a confidential fit uses its
noisy count release. Only the ordinary generation seeds are user-controlled;
there is no privacy-noise seed in `fit_private_pmx()`.

```{r minimal-generate}
mock_a <- generate_pmx(private_model, seed = 101)
mock_b <- generate_pmx(private_model, seed = 202)

identical(mock_a, mock_b)
validate_pmx(mock_a, roles, endpoints)$valid
table(mock_a$CENS)
minimal_design_check <- check_demo_similarity(
  public_source, mock_a, roles, bounds$time, "Minimal fixture"
)
knitr::kable(
  minimal_design_check, digits = 2,
  caption = "Minimal-fixture cohort and sampling-design checks"
)
```

```{r minimal-plot, eval=has_ggplot2}
minimal_comparison <- rbind(
  observed_plot_data(public_source, roles, "Source"),
  observed_plot_data(mock_a, roles, "Synthetic")
)
ggplot2::ggplot(
  minimal_comparison,
  ggplot2::aes(time, dv, group = interaction(dataset, subject),
               colour = dataset)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(alpha = 0.6) +
  ggplot2::geom_point() +
  comparison_facets(minimal_comparison) +
  ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = comparison_colours) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "TIME", y = "DV", colour = "Dataset",
    title = "Source public fixture and generated synthetic data"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

## Theophylline: repeated Q24H dose-relative PK

`theo_md` has 12 subjects and seven Q24H doses. Its source sampling is unusual:
there is an intensive profile after the first dose, one trough around the
second dose, and another intensive profile after the seventh dose. This example
does not declare either the regimen or an occasion-specific sampling schedule.
The fit learns both from bounded, privacy-accounted population summaries.
Generated actual-like collection times are jittered around generalized cells.

```{r theo-fit, eval=has_nlmixr2data}
data("theo_md", package = "nlmixr2data")
theo_roles <- pmx_roles(
  id = "ID", time = "TIME", dv = "DV", amt = "AMT",
  evid = "EVID", cmt = "CMT", covariates = "WT"
)
theo_endpoints <- list(cp = pmx_endpoint(
  alignment = "dose_relative", transform = "log", shape = "occasion",
  grid = c(0, .25, .5, 1, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 12, 23.75), cmt = 2
))
theo_model <- fit_private_pmx(
  theo_md, theo_roles, theo_endpoints, epsilon = 5, delta = 0,
  bounds = pmx_bounds(
    c(0, 170), list(cp = c(0, 30)), amt = c(0, 500),
    covariates = list(WT = c(40, 130))
  ),
  public_design = pmx_public_design(
    pmx_schema(theo_md), dose_evid = 101, dose_cmt = 1
  ),
  contribution_limits = pmx_contribution_limits(40, 8, 8, 30, 11),
  budget_allocation = pmx_budget_allocation(.1, .15, .15, .1, .5, 0),
  backend = "public", public_source = TRUE
)
theo_mock <- generate_pmx(theo_model, seed = 303)
validate_pmx(theo_mock, theo_roles, theo_endpoints)$valid
knitr::kable(
  sampling_summary(theo_model), digits = 2,
  caption = "Fitted Theophylline sampling frequency by dose occasion"
)
theo_design_check <- check_demo_similarity(
  theo_md, theo_mock, theo_roles, c(0, 170), "Theophylline"
)
knitr::kable(
  theo_design_check, digits = 2,
  caption = "Theophylline cohort and sampling-design checks"
)
```

```{r theo-plot, eval=has_nlmixr2data && has_ggplot2}
theo_comparison <- rbind(
  observed_plot_data(theo_md, theo_roles, "Source"),
  observed_plot_data(theo_mock, theo_roles, "Synthetic")
)
ggplot2::ggplot(
  theo_comparison,
  ggplot2::aes(time, dv, group = interaction(dataset, subject),
               colour = dataset)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.55, size = 0.8) +
  comparison_facets(theo_comparison) +
  ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = comparison_colours) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Study time (hours)", y = "DV", colour = "Dataset",
    title = "Theophylline source and synthetic data on study time",
    subtitle = "Dose rows remain Q24H; concentration samples occur only after doses 1, 2, and 7"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

The study-time faceted display distinguishes the Q24H dose regimen from the sampling
schedule. The fitted timing summary discovers intensive profiles around doses
1 and 7, an occasional sparse observation around dose 2, and no observations
around doses 3--6. A dose-relative display makes the coarse curve shape easier
to see:

```{r theo-tad-plot, eval=has_nlmixr2data && has_ggplot2}
theo_tad_comparison <- rbind(
  tad_plot_data(theo_md, theo_roles, "Source"),
  tad_plot_data(theo_mock, theo_roles, "Synthetic")
)
ggplot2::ggplot(
  theo_tad_comparison,
  ggplot2::aes(
    tad, dv, group = interaction(dataset, subject, occasion),
    colour = dataset
  )
) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(alpha = 0.18) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 0.7) +
  ggplot2::stat_summary(
    ggplot2::aes(x = round(tad * 4) / 4, group = dataset),
    fun = stats::median,
    geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  comparison_facets(theo_tad_comparison) +
  ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = comparison_colours) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Time after dose (hours)", y = "DV", colour = "Dataset",
    title = "Dose-relative source and synthetic profiles",
    subtitle = "Thick lines are 0.25-hour-bin medians of a coarse workflow model, not an NLME fit"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

The fitted event and timing summaries infer seven Q24H dose events and about 22
observations per subject, allocated primarily to occasions 1, 2, and 7. Neither
the regimen nor an occasion-specific sampling schedule is supplied to
generation. The dose records describe treatment; they are not seven trough
samples. Remaining profile differences are expected: the same coarse TAD curve
is reused, accumulation is not modeled, and subject/residual variability is
deliberately broad and public.

## Warfarin: separate PK and study-time PD clocks

The lower-case schema and factor levels are public declarations. `cp` uses TAD
after the dose; `pca` uses one global study-time curve and does not restart.
The synthetic cohort is generated with the same number of subjects as the
source. The figure separates source and synthetic panels while sharing a
layout across endpoints, so one cohort cannot hide the other.

```{r warfarin-fit, eval=has_nlmixr2data}
data("warfarin", package = "nlmixr2data")
warfarin_roles <- pmx_roles(
  id = "id", time = "time", dv = "dv", amt = "amt", evid = "evid",
  dvid = "dvid", covariates = c("wt", "age", "sex")
)
warfarin_endpoints <- list(
  cp = pmx_endpoint(
    "cp", "dose_relative", "log", "occasion",
    grid = c(.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 3, 6, 9, 12, 24, 48, 72, 120)
  ),
  pca = pmx_endpoint(
    "pca", "study_time", "identity", "global",
    grid = c(0, 24, 36, 48, 72, 96, 120, 144)
  )
)
warfarin_model <- fit_private_pmx(
  warfarin, warfarin_roles, warfarin_endpoints, 5, 0,
  pmx_bounds(
    c(0, 144), list(cp = c(0, 25), pca = c(0, 120)),
    amt = c(0, 200),
    covariates = list(wt = c(40, 150), age = c(18, 100))
  ),
  pmx_public_design(
    pmx_schema(warfarin), dose_times = 0, n_doses = 1,
    dose_amount = 100, dose_evid = 1,
    endpoint_grids = lapply(warfarin_endpoints, `[[`, "grid")
  ),
  pmx_contribution_limits(30, 2, 2, c(cp = 20, pca = 12), 12),
  pmx_budget_allocation(.1, .15, .15, .1, .5, 0),
  backend = "public", public_source = TRUE
)
warfarin_mock <- generate_pmx(warfarin_model, seed = 404)
c(class(warfarin_mock$id), levels(warfarin_mock$dvid))
warfarin_design_check <- check_demo_similarity(
  warfarin, warfarin_mock, warfarin_roles, c(0, 144), "Warfarin"
)
knitr::kable(
  warfarin_design_check,
  digits = 2,
  caption = "Warfarin cohort and sampling-design checks"
)
```

```{r warfarin-plot, eval=has_nlmixr2data && has_ggplot2, fig.height=7}
warfarin_comparison <- rbind(
  observed_plot_data(warfarin, warfarin_roles, "Source"),
  observed_plot_data(warfarin_mock, warfarin_roles, "Synthetic")
)
ggplot2::ggplot(
  warfarin_comparison,
  ggplot2::aes(time, dv, group = interaction(dataset, subject),
               colour = dataset)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.45, size = 0.7) +
  comparison_facets(warfarin_comparison) +
  ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = comparison_colours) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Study time", y = "DV", colour = "Dataset",
    title = "Warfarin source and synthetic, separated for comparison"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

## WBC: generalized infusion and delayed study-time response

The public design deliberately ends at 672 hours. A singleton multi-thousand-
hour source follow-up cannot be reproduced. Positive and negative infusion
records are generated as a coherent pair from a generated amount, rate, and
duration.

```{r wbc-fit, eval=has_nlmixr2data}
data("wbcSim", package = "nlmixr2data")
wbc_roles <- pmx_roles(
  id = "ID", time = "TIME", dv = "DV", amt = "AMT", evid = "EVID",
  cmt = "CMT", rate = "RATE", covariates = c("V2I", "V1I", "CLI")
)
wbc_endpoints <- list(wbc = pmx_endpoint(
  alignment = "study_time", transform = "log", shape = "global",
  grid = c(0, 72, 120, 168, 192, 240, 336, 504, 672), cmt = 3
))
wbc_model <- fit_private_pmx(
  wbcSim, wbc_roles, wbc_endpoints, 5, 0,
  pmx_bounds(
    c(0, 720), list(wbc = c(0, 30)), amt = c(-200, 200),
    rate = c(-200, 200),
    covariates = list(
      V2I = c(100, 1500), V1I = c(100, 1200), CLI = c(100, 800)
    )
  ),
  pmx_public_design(
    pmx_schema(wbcSim), dose_times = 0, n_doses = 1,
    dose_amount = 120, dose_rate = 120, infusion_duration = 3,
    dose_evid = 10101, dose_cmt = 1,
    endpoint_grids = list(wbc = wbc_endpoints$wbc$grid),
    endpoint_cmt = list(wbc = 3)
  ),
  pmx_contribution_limits(20, 2, 2, 12, 9),
  pmx_budget_allocation(.1, .15, .15, .1, .5, 0),
  backend = "public", public_source = TRUE
)
wbc_mock <- generate_pmx(wbc_model, seed = 505)
utils::head(
  wbc_mock[wbc_mock$EVID != 0,
           c("ID", "TIME", "AMT", "RATE", "CMT", "EVID")],
  12
)
wbc_design_check <- check_demo_similarity(
  wbcSim, wbc_mock, wbc_roles, c(0, 720), "WBC"
)
knitr::kable(
  wbc_design_check, digits = 2,
  caption = "WBC cohort and sampling-design checks"
)
```

```{r wbc-plot, eval=has_nlmixr2data && has_ggplot2}
wbc_comparison <- rbind(
  observed_plot_data(wbcSim, wbc_roles, "Source"),
  observed_plot_data(wbc_mock, wbc_roles, "Synthetic")
)
ggplot2::ggplot(
  wbc_comparison,
  ggplot2::aes(time, dv, group = interaction(dataset, subject),
               colour = dataset)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 0.7) +
  comparison_facets(wbc_comparison) +
  ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = comparison_colours) +
  ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 720)) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Study time", y = "DV", colour = "Dataset",
    title = "WBC source and synthetic: delayed decline, nadir, and recovery",
    subtitle = "Comparison window is the declared 0-720 hour public design"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

These displays are permissible here because all illustrated sources are public.
For confidential studies, a source-versus-mock plot is a source-derived
restricted diagnostic and must not be exported unless separately authorized
and privatized where necessary.

## Restricted comparison versus releasable validation

`validate_pmx(mock, roles, endpoints)` uses only generated data and is
post-processing. `compare_pmx(source, mock, roles, endpoints)` reads source data
and is a restricted-environment diagnostic. Its source-derived tables and plots
are explicitly marked `restricted_not_releasable`; a private mock dataset does
not privatize a new source comparison.

## Common errors

| Error | Why fitting stops | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| Missing DV, time, amount, rate, covariate, or limit bounds | Sensitivity is undefined | Establish defensible public domains before source access |
| Undeclared actual or nominal time | Event and endpoint clocks are ambiguous | Add explicit `time` and optional `nominal_time`, `tad`, and `occasion` roles |
| Date or POSIX column remains | Exact calendar information is not modeled | Explicitly exclude it and omit it from the public output schema |
| Too many summaries for a small study | Vector sensitivity and noise overwhelm utility | Shorten public grids, remove unnecessary private relationships, or use public protocol information |
| Active group has zero budget | The release cannot be accounted | Allocate a positive fraction; keep the total at or below one |
| Calling the fitter again to get another dataset | Every refit is a new composed release | Reuse the fitted model and call `generate_pmx()` with another ordinary seed |
| OpenDP is absent | Production mechanism is unavailable | Install and validate OpenDP; never switch confidential data to the public-fixture backend |

## Before export

- Confirm `privacy_report(model)$formal_dp` is `TRUE` for confidential-data
  releases and archive its ledger entry with prior releases.
- Confirm the reported epsilon, delta, justification, public inputs,
  contribution bounds, backend/version, and accounting were approved.
- Run `validate_private_model(model, strict = TRUE)` and
  `validate_pmx(mock, roles, endpoints, strict = TRUE)`.
- Inspect new IDs, endpoint facets, repeated-dose timing, infusion pairs,
  censoring boundaries, factor levels, and finite domains.
- Export neither source-derived `compare_pmx()` output nor fitting logs unless
  separately authorized and privatized where necessary.
- State that output was generated from a subject-level `(epsilon, delta)`-
  differentially private model without claiming zero risk, legal anonymity,
  scientific fidelity, or release authorization.

See `vignette("pmxSynthData-method")` for the guarantee, mechanism, sensitivity,
composition, small-study tradeoff, and implementation assumptions.
